title: sra_alignment_pipeline pipeline parameters
description: Align sequence data from the SRA to an NCBI reference genome and call variants.

required_options.title: Required options
required_options.description: Options that must be supplied for every run.

email.description: NCBI-registered email address. Required for NCBI/Entrez access.
email.help_text: Providing an email address is required when accessing the NCBI services this pipeline depends on. Register one at https://account.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov.

cpus.help_text: Per-process requests come from nf-core-style resource labels; --cpus caps them (e.g. --cpus 1 forces every task to a single core). Bounded by the CPUs your container engine is allowed to use.

sra_accession.help_text: Any SRA accession type can be used (SRR/ERR/DRR, SRX/ERX/DRX, SRP/ERP/DRP, etc.). Up to 20 SRRs associated with the accession are downloaded.

identifier.description: NCBI nucleotide identifier for the reference genome (requires --sra_accession).
identifier.help_text: Entrez nucleotide identifier for the reference genome FASTA, e.g. NC_000866.4, CP014314.1. Only one identifier can be provided and is used for all SRRs of the accession.

input_file.description: Path to a CSV with columns: sra_accession, identifier.
input_file.help_text: A CSV file with two columns (column 1: sra_accession, column 2: identifier). When provided, it takes precedence over --sra_accession/--identifier.

output_options.title: Output options
output_options.description: Where published outputs and execution reports are written.

outdir.description: Directory for all published outputs and execution reports (default: results).
outdir.help_text: Every publishDir output plus the report/timeline/trace files are written under this directory, so runs can be kept separate.

reference_cache.description: Directory used to cache downloaded reference genomes (default: <outdir>/reference_genomes).
reference_cache.help_text: Defaults to <outdir>/reference_genomes. Set this to a shared path to reuse reference downloads across runs with different --outdir values.

resource_options.title: Resource options
resource_options.description: Laptop-sane ceilings that cap what any single process may request.

max_cpus.description: Cap on CPUs any single process may request (default: 8).

max_memory.description: Cap on memory any single process may request (default: 16.GB).
max_memory.help_text: Use a Nextflow memory string such as '16.GB' or '32.GB'.

max_time.description: Cap on wall time any single process may request (default: 24.h).
max_time.help_text: Use a Nextflow duration string such as '24.h' or '2.d'.

container_options.title: Container options
container_options.description: Control which container images the pipeline resolves at runtime.

image_manifest.description: Path to the tracked container image manifest (default: conf/images.json).

container_registry.description: Override the container registry from the manifest.

container_namespace.description: Override the container namespace from the manifest.

alignment_and_variant_calling_options.title: Alignment and variant-calling options
alignment_and_variant_calling_options.description: Tuning for bowtie2 alignment and bcftools variant calling/filtering.

L.help_text: Length of the seed substrings to align during multiseed alignment. Smaller values make alignment slower but more sensitive. Must be smaller than the read length.

X.help_text: The maximum fragment length for valid paired-end alignments.

include.help_text: Providing --include or --exclude enables the run_bcftools_filter step. Use a bcftools filter expression in single quotes, e.g. 'DP>200'.

exclude.help_text: Providing --include or --exclude enables the run_bcftools_filter step. Use a bcftools filter expression in single quotes.

help.description: Show the help message (schema-generated) and exit.

container_image.description: Internal: per-process container image reference injected into each module via addParams(); not intended to be set on the command line.
